<!-- Generated by multi-agent ultra review on 2026-06-01. Scope: all of src/ except apBoost. -->

# VocaBoost Codebase Audit Report

> **Persisted audit тАФ resume here next session.** Findings were produced by a multi-agent review and each was adversarially re-verified against the source. Track progress in the **Status Tracker** below. Companion file: `audit_findings_persistence.md` (prior 2026-05-26 persistence/stability audit). Note: structured per-finding severities below come from the verifier and may differ by one level from the editorial grouping in the prose sections.

## Executive Summary

The VocaBoost codebase is functionally complete and broadly well-structured, but the review surfaced a recurring class of defects rather than isolated one-offs. The dominant systemic risks are **non-atomic Firestore read-modify-write patterns** (progress, challenges, word counters) that corrupt state under concurrency, **error-handling paths that destroy or silently swallow in-progress work** (submit failures, unhandled rejections, full-page error takeovers), and **client-side post-pagination filtering** that breaks counts and pagination. A second cluster of correctness bugs stems from **two coexisting data shapes** (legacy vs. new testId/assignment formats, 0-based vs. 1-based day indices) that are handled inconsistently across files. The simulation/dev harness contains several real bugs but is gated behind a flag, limiting production impact.

## Counts by Severity

| Blocker  | 0     |
| High     | 7     |
| Medium   | 28    |
| Low      | 25    |
| Nitpick  | 3     |
| **Total**| **63**|

---

## High

### [HIGH] submitError retry UI is unreachable (overlay only renders while isSubmitting)
**`src/pages/TypedTest.jsx:701-708, 1385-1432`**
On a save failure after retries the code calls `setSubmitError(...)` then `setIsSubmitting(false)` in the same React tick. The submitError message and "Retry Submission" button render only inside the `{isSubmitting && (...)}` overlay, so with `isSubmitting=false` they never render тАФ the user is silently returned to the test with no error and no retry.
**Fix:** Move the submitError block out of the `isSubmitting &&` gate into its own top-level conditional (mirroring the `gradingError` block at line 1435), or keep `isSubmitting` true on the save-failure path.

### [HIGH] resultsProcessedRef never reset on 'new'-test retake, skipping processTestResults
**`src/pages/TypedTest.jsx:714-722, 830-846`** (same omission in **`src/pages/MCQTest.jsx:731-743`**)
`handleRetake('new')` resets React state but not `resultsProcessedRef.current`. Since the retake stays on the same mount, the `if (!resultsProcessedRef.current)` guard skips `processTestResults` for the retake. The retake's grading is written to the attempt doc (a fresh attempt nonce) but never applied to study_states тАФ word stats/mastery fall behind the gradebook, inverting the two-phase-write invariant.
**Fix:** Set `resultsProcessedRef.current = false` in the 'new' retake branch of `handleRetake` (in both TypedTest and MCQTest) so each distinct graded attempt is processed.

### [HIGH] Student progress never loads for legacy (assignedLists-array) classes
**`src/pages/ClassDetail.jsx:186-200`**
`loadMembers` gates the progress fetch on `classInfo?.assignments` and derives listIds via `Object.keys(classInfo.assignments)`, but the component explicitly supports the legacy `classInfo.assignedLists` array shape (see `loadAssignedLists` and `availableLists`). For legacy classes `assignments` is undefined, so `studentProgressMap` is never populated and every student shows "Not started."
**Fix:** Derive `const listIds = classInfo.assignedLists || Object.keys(classInfo.assignments || {})` and gate on `listIds.length`, matching the dual-shape pattern already used twice in the file.

### [HIGH] Submit failure destroys the in-progress test by switching to full-page error view
**`src/pages/BlindSpotCheck.jsx:118-135, 151-163`**
`handleSubmit` catches a failed `processTestResults` with `setError(...)`. The full-page error early-return (`if (error) return ...`) precedes the test render, so any transient submit failure replaces the entire test UI with a "Back to Dashboard" screen тАФ up to 30 collected answers become inaccessible with no retry.
**Fix:** Use a separate inline `submitError` state shown near the Submit button, keep the test UI mounted to allow retry, and reserve the full-page `error` for initial load failures only.

### [HIGH] reviewChallenge recalculates score with wrong denominator (answers.length vs totalQuestions)
**`src/services/db.js:2615-2616`**
The original score uses `correctCount / totalQuestions` (`submitTestAttempt`), where `totalQuestions` can exceed `answers.length` for skipped/partial submissions. `reviewChallenge` recomputes `correctCount / updatedAnswers.length`, a smaller denominator, so reviewing a challenge on any skipped-question attempt silently inflates the score (and can flip `passed` falseтЖТtrue). The recalculation runs even on rejection.
**Fix:** Use the persisted denominator: `const denom = attemptData.totalQuestions || updatedAnswers.length; const newScore = Math.round((correctCount / denom) * 100)`.

### [HIGH] Transient Firestore read failure during auth init demotes user to 'student', locking teachers out
**`src/contexts/AuthContext.jsx:45-51`**
`loadProfile` catches any `getDoc` error and sets `{ ...firebaseUser, role: 'student' }`, discarding profile/stats/settings. This `getDoc` is the raw firebase import, not the `withRetry` wrapper, so a transient network/offline/rules-latency error during auth init redirects a real teacher away from every teacher route (`TeacherRoute.jsx:15`).
**Fix:** On read failure, retry via the db.js retry helper, surface an error state, or preserve a previously known role тАФ distinguish "role unknown due to error" from "role is student"; never grant/deny route access from a failed read.

### [HIGH] hasTestToday checks attempts across ALL lists, corrupting per-list daily status
**`src/pages/Dashboard.jsx:1256`**
`panelCState` is per-list, but `testCompletedToday = hasTestToday(userAttempts)` where `userAttempts` is the student's global attempt set (no list filter). Completing a test on any other list today flips the primary focus list's `dailyStatus` to 'completed', hiding its "needs test" prompt. The adjacent session check (`hasSessionToday(progress?.recentSessions)`) is correctly per-list, making this inconsistent.
**Fix:** Filter `userAttempts` to the primary focus list (by `attempt.listId === getPrimaryFocus.id`, ideally also matching classId) before calling `hasTestToday`.

---

## Medium

### [MEDIUM] updateClassProgress read-modify-write is not atomic (day double-advance / duplicate sessions)
**`src/services/progressService.js:327-369`**
`getDoc` тЖТ compute `currentStudyDay+1`, append to `recentSessions`, add `wordsIntroduced` тЖТ `updateDoc`, with no transaction. Two concurrent invocations (duplicate tab, double-click, offline replay, partial retry) both read the same value and both write, double-advancing the day, duplicating sessions, and double-counting words. The in-function day guard is a check-then-act on the same stale snapshot and does not protect against overlap.
**Fix:** Wrap the read+write in `runTransaction` re-checking the day guard inside, or use `FieldValue.increment` for counters and key writes by `sessionSummary.day` for idempotency.

### [MEDIUM] moveToReviewPhase fetches failed-new-word docs without error handling
**`src/pages/DailySessionFlow.jsx:944-959`**
`Promise.all` over raw `getDoc` for each failed id with no try/catch. Called from `handleContinueToReview` (no surrounding handling), so a rejection becomes an unhandled rejection тАФ the Continue button does nothing, no error UI shows, and the user is stuck. Bypasses the db.js retry wrapper. (Note: the `getSegmentWords` call on line 937 is similarly unguarded.)
**Fix:** Wrap the segment/failed-word loads in try/catch with an error state; prefer the retry-enabled db.js helpers over raw `getDoc`.

### [MEDIUM] Off-by-one: 0-based completedDays compared against 1-based expectedDay
**`src/pages/Dashboard.jsx:186-194`**
`completedDays = currentStudyDay` (0-based) is subtracted from `expectedDay = calculateExpectedStudyDay(...)` (1-based). A new on-track user on day 1 yields `0 - 1 = -1`, rendering "1 behind" while the same component shows "Day 1". The pace indicator is systematically one day pessimistic.
**Fix:** Compare like-for-like: `difference = (completedDays + 1) - expectedDay`, or compare `completedDays` against `expectedDay - 1`.

### [MEDIUM] loadStudentClasses swallows/leaks errors with no catch and no error state
**`src/pages/Dashboard.jsx:526-541`**
`try/finally` with no `catch`. A `fetchStudentClasses` rejection produces an unhandled rejection (invoked from an effect) and leaves the empty/loading UI with no failure indication. Every other loader in the file has a catch.
**Fix:** Add a catch that logs and sets a user-visible error state.

### [MEDIUM] "Pending Challenge" badge can never render тАФ list query strips answers
**`src/pages/Gradebook.jsx:1108`** (root cause **`src/services/db.js:1996`**)
The badge gates on `attempt.answers?.some(a => a.challengeStatus === 'pending')`, but `queryTeacherAttempts` returns `answers: []` for every row (lazy-loaded). The `.some(...)` is always false, so teachers never see which attempts have pending challenges in the list view.
**Fix:** Have `queryTeacherAttempts` compute `hasPendingChallenge` from the real answers before discarding them, and gate the badge on that flag.

### [MEDIUM] Server-side post-filtering breaks row count and 'more available' indicator
**`src/pages/Gradebook.jsx:933-936`** (and **`src/services/db.js:1915, 1944-1958`**) тАФ *see also the queryStudentAttempts/queryTeacherAttempts entry below; same root cause*
Name/List/Test Type filters are applied as post-fetch `continue` skips, but `hasMore = (docCount === pageSize)` is computed on the raw pre-filter count and `lastVisible` is the last raw doc. Result: undercounted "Showing N", near-empty pages while `hasMore` is true, and select-all over only the sparse loaded subset.
**Fix:** Push filters into the Firestore query where feasible, or page internally until `pageSize` matching rows accumulate, basing `hasMore`/cursor on raw pages remaining.

### [MEDIUM] queryStudentAttempts/queryTeacherAttempts apply filters after the pagination limit
**`src/services/db.js:2094-2147`** (teacher equivalent `1906-1958`)
`limit(pageSize)` is applied server-side, then List/Name/TestType filters (and a `!listId` skip) run as `continue` on the page, while `hasMore` and `lastVisible` derive from the raw count. Pages render sparse/empty, cursors advance over filtered-out rows, and "Load more" behaves inconsistently when a filter is active.
**Fix:** Move filters into the query (listId/testType are derivable/stored), or implement a fill-page loop and base `hasMore`/`lastVisible` on post-filter results. (Shares the root cause of the Gradebook count entry above тАФ fix together.)

### [MEDIUM] Simulation auto-answer reads word.text but word objects use word.word
**`src/pages/TypedTest.jsx:163-175, 628-634`**
The sim auto-answer generator reads `word.text`, but word docs expose `.word` (used everywhere in the real submit path). The correct branch assigns `undefined`; the typo branch evaluates `undefined.length`, throwing a TypeError. Simulation runs crash or grade everything incorrect.
**Fix:** Use `word.word` (or `word.text || word.word`) in the simulation answer generator.

### [MEDIUM] MCQ auto-answer effect double-increments currentIndex, skipping every other question
**`src/pages/MCQTest.jsx:131-144`**
In sim auto-mode the effect calls `handleAnswerSelect`, which already advances the index, then advances it again. Both are functional updaters in the same callback, so the index jumps by 2 тАФ skipped words get no answer and score as incorrect, corrupting sim results.
**Fix:** Remove the second increment; only handle the final-question submit branch, or factor out a no-advance answer setter for sim use.

### [MEDIUM] Gradebook answerArray uses async `answers` state while scoring uses synced ref
**`src/pages/MCQTest.jsx:458-517`**
`results`/`summary` (displayed score + study_state mutations) derive from `answersRef.current`, but `answerArray` (the gradebook payload) is built from the `answers` state. On sim auto-submit, `handleSubmit` runs from a setTimeout bound to the pre-final-answer render, so its `answers` closure omits the last answer while the ref includes it тАФ split-brain between displayed and recorded score.
**Fix:** Build `answerArray` from `Object.entries(answersRef.current)` so payload, summary, and study_state mutations share one source of truth.

### [MEDIUM] Recovery resume restores answers/currentIndex without validating against regenerated testWords
**`src/pages/MCQTest.jsx:382-394`**
`handleRecoveryResume` restores saved `answers` and `currentIndex` with no validation, but `testWords` were freshly generated (reshuffled, possibly a different subset). Restored answers for missing wordIds are silently dropped (and new words score incorrect); a `currentIndex` past the shorter array makes `currentWord` undefined and can crash. A `validateTestState(testId, currentWordIds)` helper already exists in `utils/testRecovery.js` but is unused here.
**Fix:** Validate saved wordIds against `testWords.map(w=>w.id)` (use the existing helper), clamp `currentIndex` to `[0, testWords.length-1]`, and discard recovery if the word set no longer matches.

### [MEDIUM] Every class mutation re-fetches all members + per-student aggregate stats (N+1)
**`src/pages/ClassDetail.jsx:170-201, 242-251`**
`loadMembers` depends on `classInfo?.assignments`; every mutating handler calls `loadClass()`, producing a new `classInfo` object that re-runs the effect. This re-fetches the whole roster, calls `fetchStudentAggregateStats` per member (each a full study_states subcollection scan), and `fetchStudentsProgressForClass` (students├Чlists getDocs) тАФ even on a settings save that changed no membership.
**Fix:** Separate roster loading from progress loading; trigger `loadMembers` on `classId` only and progress fetch on the list set, or skip when membership is unchanged.

### [MEDIUM] CSV export vulnerable to delimiter breakage and formula injection
**`src/pages/ClassDetail.jsx:462-468`**
`handleExportSelectedStudents` joins `displayName`/`email` with no escaping. A comma/quote/newline corrupts row alignment; a value starting with `=`/`+`/`-`/`@` is interpreted as a formula in Excel/Sheets (CSV injection). These fields are user-controlled student display names.
**Fix:** Escape each field тАФ wrap in double quotes, double internal quotes, and prefix a `'` or tab to neutralize formula-leading characters.

### [MEDIUM] Deleting a word leaves position gaps and causes duplicate positions on next add
**`src/pages/ListEditor.jsx:173-187`** (root cause **`src/services/db.js:589-599, 531-545`**)
`deleteWord` only does `increment(-1)`; it does not re-sequence `position`. New words get `position = currentCount (== wordCount)`, so after a middle delete (positions 0,1,3,4, wordCount=4) the next add collides at position 4. With `orderBy('position','asc')`, duplicate positions sort unstably and downstream position-dependent features are affected.
**Fix:** Re-sequence positions after delete in a batch, or derive `position` from `max(position)+1`, or order by `createdAt`. Make delete+reindex atomic in db.js.

### [MEDIUM] wordCount and word list desync when add/delete partially fails (no transaction)
**`src/pages/ListEditor.jsx:181-186, 227-247`** (root cause **`src/services/db.js:550-554, 594-598`**)
Word doc write and the parent-list `wordCount` update are two separate unbatched writes. If the second fails after the first, `wordCount` drifts permanently and `ListLibrary` renders the wrong denormalized count. Concurrent adds also race on `position` derived from read-then-write of `wordCount`.
**Fix:** Wrap the word write and parent-list counter update in a `writeBatch`/`runTransaction`.

### [MEDIUM] "Remaining blind spots" count double-counts just-failed words
**`src/pages/BlindSpotCheck.jsx:255`**
`remainingBlindSpots = blindSpotPool.length - questions.length + results.failed.length`. A just-FAILED word is excluded from a freshly reloaded pool (not NEVER_TESTED, `lastTestedAt` is now), yet the expression re-adds `results.failed.length`, so the displayed count disagrees with the actual next pool fetched by "Test More Blind Spots".
**Fix:** Compute `remaining = blindSpotPool.length - questions.length`, or re-read the fresh pool count.

### [MEDIUM] MCQ generation can produce fewer than 4 options when the blind spot pool is small
**`src/pages/BlindSpotCheck.jsx:85-107`**
Distractors are drawn from the blind spot subset and sliced to 3; a pool of 1тАУ3 words yields 1тАУ3 options (a 1-word pool renders only the correct answer). No de-duplication of identical definitions either. The only guard is `length === 0`, but 1тАУ3 word pools are normal.
**Fix:** Pad distractors from the full word list, dedupe option text against the correct definition, and optionally require a minimum pool size.

### [MEDIUM] submitChallenge/reviewChallenge perform read-modify-write on arrays without a transaction
**`src/services/db.js:2516-2554`**
Both read `attempt.answers` / `challenges.history`, mutate in JS, and write the whole array back via `updateDoc` with no transaction. Concurrent operations overwrite each other (lost challenge/answer mutation), and the token-availability check computed from a stale snapshot lets concurrent challenges exceed the limit.
**Fix:** Wrap the read-modify-write of answers/history in `runTransaction` so arrays are re-read inside the transaction and contended writes are rejected.

### [MEDIUM] resetStudentProgress only deletes attempts in the new testId format
**`src/services/db.js:2872-2883`**
The reset filters by `parts.length >= 5 && parts[3] === listId` (new `vocaboost_test_{classId}_{listId}_{sessionType}` shape). The file handles a legacy `test_/typed_{listId}_{timestamp}` format elsewhere; those split into 3 parts, fail the check, and are never deleted, contradicting the "delete all attempts for the class/list" contract.
**Fix:** Reuse the dual-format regex extraction used elsewhere in the file instead of the positional `parts[3]` check.

### [MEDIUM] Per-class member fetch failures silently drop students from teacher gradebook aggregates
**`src/services/db.js:1498-1515`** (identical at **`1773-1794`**)
A `getDocs` failure on a class members subcollection is caught, `console.error`'d, and iteration continues. The attempt query is built from `studentIdSet`; a transient member-fetch failure omits those students' attempts with no error surfaced тАФ a silently incomplete gradebook.
**Fix:** Wrap member queries in `withRetry`, or aggregate failures and signal a partial/error state to the caller.

### [MEDIUM] Unbounded full-list reads on every session init and PDF/debug call
**`src/services/studyService.js:278-308`** (same pattern in `getNewWords`, `getFailedFromPreviousNewWords`, `getBlindSpotPool`)
These do `getDocs(query(wordsRef, orderBy('position')))` with no limit, pulling the entire words subcollection then filtering client-side. `initializeDailySession` (invoked by PDF/debug paths) calls `getSegmentWords` several times per flow, so a single render can read the full list 3тАУ4 times.
**Fix:** Use server-side range queries: `where('position','>=',start)`, `where('position','<=',end)`, `orderBy('position')`; use `where + limit(count)` for `getNewWords`.

### [MEDIUM] graduateSegmentWords can graduate never-tested words to MASTERED
**`src/services/studyService.js:861-902`**
`getSegmentWords` synthesizes a default NEVER_TESTED state for words lacking one. `graduateSegmentWords` treats every segment word not in `failedWordIds` as eligible and randomly graduates `floor(segmentSize * testScore)` to MASTERED тАФ so a never-tested word can be flipped to MASTERED and hidden for 21 days. `segmentSize` is the raw index span, over-counting when the range includes untested words.
**Fix:** Restrict eligibility to actually-tested words (exclude NEVER_TESTED), and base the count on the eligible word count, not the index span.

### [MEDIUM] fetchStudentsProgressForClass issues one getDoc per student per list (N+1 fan-out)
**`src/services/progressService.js:410-421`**
For S students ├Ч L lists, builds S├ЧL individual `getClassProgress` getDocs via one `Promise.all` (e.g. 150 reads for 30 students ├Ч 5 lists) on every teacher load, with no batching.
**Fix:** Batch with `getAll`/`in`-queries keyed by `classId_listId` docId, or a `collectionGroup` query filtered by classId; chunk concurrency.

### [MEDIUM] signup writes role: undefined to Firestore when no role is provided
**`src/contexts/AuthContext.jsx:75-82`** (interaction with **`src/services/db.js:220, 228`**)
`signup` passes `role: gradData.role` (gradData defaults to `{}`), so role can be `undefined`. In db.js `userDocument = { role: docOverrides.role ?? 'student', ..., ...docOverrides }` тАФ the trailing spread re-applies `role: undefined` after the fallback. With `ignoreUndefinedProperties` off, `setDoc` rejects, hard-failing account creation. The setUser call at line 87 correctly uses `?? 'student'`.
**Fix:** Pass `role: gradData.role ?? 'student'` from signup, or strip undefined keys / move the `?? 'student'` after the spread in db.js.

### [MEDIUM] getAutoAnswer can infinite-loop when optionsCount <= 1
**`src/hooks/useSimulation.jsx:226-230`**
The wrong-answer `do/while` loops until it finds an index тЙа `correctIndex`. For a single-option question (reachable via a one-word list), the only candidate (0) equals `correctIndex` (0), so the loop never terminates and the tab hangs. Sim-mode only.
**Fix:** Guard `optionsCount <= 1` before the loop, or build an explicit `wrongs[]` array and fall back to `correctIndex` when empty.

### [MEDIUM] Auto-swipe recursion not stopped by pause/state changes (stale closure)
**`src/hooks/useSimulation.jsx:258-271`**
`triggerAutoSwipe` schedules a setTimeout that recursively re-calls the closure captured at timer creation (phase=RUNNING). If an already-fired callback runs after `pauseSimulation` cleared the ref, it re-schedules a new timer whose stale guard still sees RUNNING тАФ pause fails to stop the loop. Sim-mode only.
**Fix:** Read live `phase`/`isAutoMode` from refs inside the callback and re-check before re-scheduling, or drive continuation from a `useEffect` keyed on phase.

### [MEDIUM] buildTestConfig caps review test at static testSizeReview, ignoring intervention-scaled size
**`src/utils/testConfig.js:40-44`**
Review `effectiveTestSize` is the static `testSizeReview` (default 30), but the review queue is built upstream with intervention-scaled counts (up to 60). A struggling student's enlarged review queue is silently truncated to 30, defeating the adaptive mechanic; `studyAlgorithm` and `sessionTimeCalculator` assume the scaled size, so the three modules disagree.
**Fix:** Compute review `effectiveTestSize` via `calculateReviewTestSize(interventionLevel, min, max)` (pass `interventionLevel` into `buildTestConfig`, already available in sessionContext).

---

## Low

### [LOW] Auto-swipe simulation effect has stale closures and leaks nested setTimeouts
**`src/pages/DailySessionFlow.jsx:178-246`**
The effect's nested `setTimeout` calls (lines 227-236) calling `goToNewWordTest`/`handleFinishReviewStudy` are not tracked; cleanup only clears the outer `autoSwipeTimerRef`, so on phase change/unmount those callbacks still fire (navigation after unmount). Dep array omits `sim` and the handlers (largely benign since session config is set once). Sim-mode only.
**Fix:** Store the nested timeout in a second ref and clear it in cleanup; stabilize the handlers or include them in deps.

### [LOW] handleLocalRecoveryContinue clamps currentIndex to studyQueue length, not the restored queue
**`src/pages/DailySessionFlow.jsx:1434-1460`**
The restored queue can be shorter than the saved `studyQueue` (some saved ids no longer in the reloaded pool), but `currentIndex` is clamped to `studyQueue.length - 1`. If `restoredQueue` is shorter, `currentWord` is undefined and the "All cards reviewed!" empty state renders prematurely.
**Fix:** Clamp against `restoredQueue.length - 1`.

### [LOW] handleListSelection: unhandled rejection on settings write
**`src/pages/Dashboard.jsx:277-288`**
The onClick awaits `updateUserSettings` (no `withRetry`, no internal catch) with no try/catch, so a failed write is an unhandled rejection with no user feedback. (The optimistic state update is after the await, so it does not run on rejection тАФ no false-success divergence.)
**Fix:** Wrap in try/catch and surface an error on failure.

### [LOW] 7-Day Rhythm includes today even when today is a skipped weekend
**`src/pages/Dashboard.jsx:1202-1233`**
With `skipWeekends`, the loop seeds `currentDate = today` and only skips weekends when stepping backward, so the first bar uses today unconditionally тАФ on Sat/Sun it shows a weekend that should be excluded.
**Fix:** Before the loop, advance `currentDate` off any initial weekend when `skipWeekends`.

### [LOW] Previous-page button resets to page 1 instead of back one page
**`src/pages/Gradebook.jsx:408-431`**
`loadNextPage` appends cumulatively; `loadPreviousPage` discards all loaded rows and re-fetches page 1, hard-setting `currentPage` to 1. The ChevronLeft affordance dumps a teacher who loaded pages 1тАУ4 back to page 1. (Comments indicate this is intentional, but it mismatches the cumulative model.)
**Fix:** Pick one model тАФ slice the loaded array and decrement on back, or keep a stack of page cursors.

### [LOW] answeredCount assumes every response is a string; non-string throws r.trim
**`src/pages/TypedTest.jsx:923`**
`Object.values(responses).filter(r => r.trim() !== '')` runs every render. Recovery restores `responses` from unvalidated localStorage; a non-string value (corrupt/legacy payload) makes `r.trim()` throw and crashes the test page render.
**Fix:** Guard: `.filter(r => typeof r === 'string' && r.trim() !== '')`.

### [LOW] Debug console.log statements left in production submit path
**`src/pages/MCQTest.jsx:468-478, 525-551, 559-572`** (related: **`src/components/ImportWordsModal.jsx:195`** below)
Unconditional `DEBUG MCQ Results` / `[DEBUG STUDYDAY]` / `[SUBMIT]` logs on every submission dump uid, per-question results, and session metadata to the console. (Client-side own-data, so log noise/hygiene rather than true PII exfiltration.)
**Fix:** Remove or gate behind an explicit debug flag.

### [LOW] Bulk student removal aborts midway on error, leaving partial state and stale UI
**`src/pages/ClassDetail.jsx:437-457`**
A sequential `for`-loop of awaits; if removal throws mid-batch, the loop stops, `setSelectedStudents([])` and `loadClass()` never run, and the UI shows the old roster with all still selected. (Deletion is idempotent, so retry is safe.)
**Fix:** Use `Promise.allSettled`, report failures, and always refresh + reset selection in a `finally`.

### [LOW] navigator.clipboard.writeText rejection is unhandled
**`src/pages/ClassDetail.jsx:518-524`**
`writeText` is not awaited/caught; the Clipboard API rejects in insecure contexts, on denied permission, or when unfocused. The optimistic "copied" feedback shows regardless, plus an unhandled rejection.
**Fix:** Await/`.then` and set success only on resolve; show a fallback message on `.catch`.

### [LOW] Review test Min/Max not validated as min <= max
**`src/pages/ClassDetail.jsx:1102-1140, 355-379`** (also `src/services/db.js:860-874`)
Min and Max are clamped 1..500 independently with no cross-field check, so a teacher can save min=60, max=30. Downstream (`sessionTimeCalculator.js:80`) then scales review size *downward* as intervention increases.
**Fix:** Enforce `reviewTestSizeMin <= reviewTestSizeMax` before save (clamp or block with a message).

### [LOW] Operator precedence bug: add-word error always shows "Unable to update word."
**`src/pages/ListEditor.jsx:249`**
`err.message ?? editingId ? 'Unable to update word.' : 'Unable to add word.'` parses as `(err.message ?? editingId) ? ...`. With a truthy `err.message`, it always selects "update," even when adding.
**Fix:** Parenthesize: `err.message ?? (editingId ? 'Unable to update word.' : 'Unable to add word.')`.

### [LOW] Stale word list after edit when reload throws (success shown but UI not refreshed)
**`src/pages/ListEditor.jsx:218-247`**
Success state is set and the form reset before the words re-fetch. If the write succeeds but the reload `getDocs` throws, the catch overwrites with an error while the form is already cleared and the table shows pre-edit data тАФ both a success and error banner appear.
**Fix:** Reload words before resetting the form, or treat reload failure separately (keep success, show a non-blocking "refresh" note).

### [LOW] Unhandled rejection in "Test More Blind Spots" handler leaves stale pool, no feedback
**`src/pages/BlindSpotCheck.jsx:296-308`**
The async onClick awaits `getBlindSpotPool` with no try/catch. On failure, `testStarted=false`/`results=null` are already set but the pool isn't refreshed and no error shows, so the pre-test screen renders the old pool. (UI stays interactive тАФ not a hang.)
**Fix:** Wrap in try/catch with inline error; refresh the pool before clearing results, or show a loading state.

### [LOW] loadClasses swallows errors, showing 'No classes enrolled' on failure
**`src/pages/Settings.jsx:36-43`**
A `fetchStudentClasses` failure only `console.error`s; `studentClasses` stays `[]`, rendering "No classes enrolled." тАФ indistinguishable from a genuine empty state.
**Fix:** Set an error state and render a distinct "Failed to load тАФ retry" message.

### [LOW] Email/password login double-navigates and can land on the wrong route
**`src/pages/Login.jsx:22-30, 41-53`**
`handleSubmit` calls `navigate(redirectTo)` while the user-watching effect also navigates (with `@apboost.test` тЖТ `/ap` special-casing). The two race and compute different targets, producing a flicker/redundant navigation. The Google flow already relies solely on the effect.
**Fix:** Remove the explicit `navigate` from `handleSubmit` and let the effect handle all redirects.

### [LOW] Signup Google sign-in ignores apBoost redirect and always lands on '/'
**`src/pages/Signup.jsx:49-66`** (lines 41, 56)
Both `handleSubmit` and `handleGoogleSignIn` hardcode `navigate('/')`, unlike Login which routes apBoost users to `/ap`. An apBoost user signing up lands on the vocaBoost root.
**Fix:** Mirror Login's redirect logic (compute target from email domain/location).

### [LOW] Graduation year/month accepted without real validation
**`src/pages/Signup.jsx:36-37, 156-179`**
`gradYear`/`gradMonth` are sent as raw `Number(value)` with only HTML `min`/`max` attributes and no JS range check before `createUserDocument`. Native form validation blocks ordinary out-of-range submits, but programmatic/non-form paths can persist invalid dates.
**Fix:** Validate ranges (month 1тАУ12, year window) in `handleSubmit`; ideally enforce server-side / in Firestore rules.

### [LOW] submitTestAttempt divides by totalQuestions which defaults to 0 (NaN score)
**`src/services/db.js:1123-1151`**
`score = correct / totalQuestions` with `totalQuestions = 0` default and no denominator guard. Latent: current callers pass `testWords.length`, but the default plus missing guard is a corruption path if a caller omits/passes 0.
**Fix:** `const denom = totalQuestions > 0 ? totalQuestions : answeredWords.length; score = denom > 0 ? correct/denom : 0` (or throw when `<= 0`).

### [LOW] fetchStudentStats conflates 'tested' with 'mastered' in the headline percentage
**`src/services/db.js:1027-1075`**
The `mastery` percentage uses `masteryCount` (PASSED + FAILED, i.e. tested at least once), while the returned `masteredWords`/`masteryCount` field is overridden with PASSED-only. A failed word inflates the headline percentage but not the count. (Function currently has no in-repo caller; the title's "across ALL lists" claim is inaccurate тАФ line 1039 correctly restricts to the list.)
**Fix:** Base the `mastery` percentage on `masteredWords` (PASSED) if it means mastery, or rename the field to "words attempted."

### [LOW] fetchStudentStats/fetchStudentAggregateStats read the entire study_states collection unbounded
**`src/services/db.js:1028-1037`**
Both `getDocs` the full per-user `study_states` collection with no limit/`where`, then filter in JS via `wordIds.includes(...)` (O(N┬╖M)). `fetchStudentStats` does this once per list.
**Fix:** Filter server-side with `where('listId','==',listId)` (study_states already store listId) and use a `Set` for membership.

### [LOW] fetchStudentClasses N+1 reads per assigned list and in-place mutation of snapshot data
**`src/services/db.js:449-489`**
Nested `getDoc` per class then per assigned list (no batching). Also mutates `classData.assignments[key].testMode` in place; harmless but duplicates `fetchClass`'s defaulting logic, which has already drifted (fetchClass also defaults `testOptionsCount` and converts arrays).
**Fix:** Batch list reads (chunked `where(documentId(),'in',...)` or a shared cache) and centralize assignment defaulting in one helper used by both.

### [LOW] getBlindSpotPool caches count via updateDoc which throws if the doc doesn't exist
**`src/services/studyService.js:693-706`**
`updateDoc(progressRef, {...})` rejects with "No document to update" when the `class_progress` doc isn't yet created; it's swallowed by try/catch, so the cache write silently never succeeds and `getBlindSpotCount` always falls back to full recomputation. (Currently latent тАФ no caller passes `classId`.)
**Fix:** Use `setDoc(progressRef, {...}, { merge: true })`.

### [LOW] Orphaned-review cleanup only inspects the 8 most recent attempts
**`src/services/progressService.js:163-168`**
`cleanupOrphanedReviews` is fed only the 8 most-recent attempts (`getRecentAttemptsForClassList(..., 8)`), so orphan reviews outside that window are never deleted and persist. (Impact limited: CSD derives from an exact-day query, so undeleted orphans-beyond-anchor don't corrupt CSD тАФ dead rows, not active corruption.)
**Fix:** Query orphan candidates directly (`reviews where studyDay > anchorDay`) rather than the 8-row window.

### [LOW] calculateUpdatedStreak mutates the passed-in Date via setHours
**`src/services/progressService.js:293-296`**
When `lastStudyDate` is a Date instance it isn't cloned before `setHours(0,0,0,0)`, mutating the caller's object. Currently harmless (the sole caller passes a fresh `.toDate()`), but a future shared-Date caller would be corrupted. `getExpectedPreviousDay` clones correctly.
**Fix:** Always clone before mutating: `const lastDate = new Date(lastStudyDate instanceof Date ? lastStudyDate.getTime() : (lastStudyDate?.toDate?.() ?? lastStudyDate))`.

### [LOW] Theme effect always persists to localStorage, making the system-preference listener dead code
**`src/contexts/ThemeContext.jsx:112-125`**
The effect writes `vocaboost-theme` on every run including mount, so the listener's `if (!stored)` branch is never reached тАФ "follow system preference" silently never updates on OS theme changes.
**Fix:** Use a separate explicit-choice sentinel flag (set only in user theme setters) and gate the system listener on that flag, persisting `vocaboost-theme` only on explicit choice.

### [LOW] First-time Google sign-in can race onAuthStateChanged, persisting an empty profile/role
**`src/contexts/AuthContext.jsx:103-129`**
`signInWithGoogle` creates the user doc only after the popup resolves and never force-updates context. Meanwhile `onAuthStateChanged`'s `loadProfile` can read the not-yet-created doc, setting `{}` profile/null stats and role 'student'. The email signup path handles this race (lines 84-95) but Google doesn't.
**Fix:** After creating the doc for a first-time OAuth user, force-update context state as the email path does, or create-then-read in `onAuthStateChanged`.

### [LOW] onSessionComplete setTimeout is never cleared (navigate after stop/unmount)
**`src/hooks/useSimulation.jsx:127-131`**
The 500ms timer calling `navigateRef.current(...)` isn't stored in a ref or cleared in stop/reset/unmount. Stopping/unmounting within the window still navigates (re-entering the session loop via a captured non-null `sessionTarget`). Sim-mode only.
**Fix:** Store the id in a ref and `clearTimeout` it in `stopSimulation`, `resetSimulation`, and the unmount cleanup.

### [LOW] completeDay's day-scoped issue filter misses warnings/errors lacking dayNumber
**`src/hooks/useSimulationLog.js:140`**
`completeDay` filters issues by `i.context?.dayNumber === dayNumber`, but `logMismatch` (called by `validateExpectation` with `{ check, tolerance }` and no `dayNumber`) never sets it. Per-day summaries under-report failures while the global pass-rate counts them. Dev tooling only.
**Fix:** Include the current `dayNumber` in every `logMismatch`/`logError` context, or capture issues recorded since the day started.

### [LOW] Event/issue ids use Date.now() and collide, breaking React keys
**`src/hooks/useSimulationLog.js:81, 99`**
`logEvent` uses `Date.now() + Math.random()`, but `logError`/`logMismatch` use plain `Date.now()`. Issues created in the same millisecond share ids, producing duplicate React keys in the dev log panel. Dev tooling only.
**Fix:** Use the same id scheme (`Date.now() + Math.random()`), a counter, or `crypto.randomUUID()`.

### [LOW] 'both' test-mode time estimate double-counts the middle question for odd sizes
**`src/utils/sessionTimeCalculator.js:68-69`**
`halfSize = Math.ceil(size/2)` is used for both MCQ and typed halves, so for odd sizes (e.g. 51 тЖТ 26+26=52) the estimate is inflated by one question. Display estimate only.
**Fix:** `const mcqHalf = Math.ceil(size/2); const typedHalf = Math.floor(size/2);` and use each respectively.

### [LOW] PDF definition cell ignores rich fallbacks whenever word.definition is present
**`src/utils/pdfGenerator.js:137-143`**
`definitionText` only appends `definitions.ko`, and `buildDefinitionCell` (which handles all languages, `definitionKo`, secondary defs, etc.) is used only when `definitionText` is empty. A word with `definition` set but translations in `definitions.es`/`.fr`/`definitionKo` silently drops them from the PDF.
**Fix:** Use `buildDefinitionCell` as the single source of truth for the definition column.

### [LOW] speak() leaves utterance handlers/promise dangling on browser silent-failure
**`src/utils/tts.js:23-36`**
`speak()` resolves only on `onend`, rejects only on `onerror`. When speechSynthesis silently drops an utterance (rapid `cancel()`, Chrome >15s pause bug), neither fires, so `await speak(...)` never settles, the `finally` in callers never runs, and the in-flight audio guard stays true permanently тАФ disabling audio for that component instance.
**Fix:** Add a bounded safety timeout that settles the promise and detaches handlers on settle.

### [LOW] MasteryBars fully fills the grid from masteredCount when totalWords is 0
**`src/components/MasteryBars.jsx:8-16`**
When `totalWords=0` but `masteredCount>0`, `wordsPerSquare` falls back to 1, filling the 28-square grid, while `masteryPercent` shows 0% тАФ the visual contradicts the numeric.
**Fix:** When `safeTotalWords === 0`, render zero filled squares.

### [LOW] ImportWordsModal debug console.log leaks imported word contents
**`src/components/ImportWordsModal.jsx:195`**
`console.log('ImportWordsModal тЖТ Sending to DB:', wordsToImport[0])` runs unguarded on every import, logging the first word object's full structure in production. (Logs the first word only; user's own data, so noise more than exfiltration.)
**Fix:** Remove the line.

### [LOW] Disabled CardButton div lacks aria-disabled and never gets dimmed styling
**`src/components/ui/buttons/CardButton.jsx:50-67`**
The div branch (used when disabled) carries `disabled:opacity-60 disabled:cursor-not-allowed`, but Tailwind's `disabled:` variant maps to `:disabled`, which a `<div>` can never match тАФ so the dim/cursor styling never applies. It also omits `aria-disabled`, so AT announces it as enabled. (The title's "Modal/onClose dialog" prefix is copy-paste noise; the body correctly targets CardButton.)
**Fix:** Add `aria-disabled={disabled}` and apply dim/cursor via a plain conditional class instead of the `disabled:` variant.

---

## Nitpick

### [NITPICK] Clearing a numeric settings field silently resets to a default instead of the prior value
**`src/pages/ClassDetail.jsx:969-983`**
`onBlur` does `parseInt(e.target.value,10) || 20`; since `parseInt('')` is NaN, clearing the Pace field snaps it to the factory default 20 (not the list's configured value). Repeats for `testOptionsCount` (||4), `studyDaysPerWeek` (||5), `passThreshold` (||95), `testSizeNew` (||50), and review sizes.
**Fix:** On empty/invalid input, fall back to the current `settingsForm`/list value rather than a fixed default.

### [NITPICK] setState after await with no unmount guard in async effect and reload
**`src/pages/BlindSpotCheck.jsx:50-71, 297-302`**
The `load()` effect and "Test More" handler call setState after awaited Firestore calls with no mounted guard. (React 18+ removed the unmounted-setState warning, so this is now a wasted-work no-op rather than a warning.)
**Fix:** Add a `let cancelled = false` flag with cleanup and guard the setState calls.

### [NITPICK] StudySelectionModal stats block renders based on always-truthy stats object
**`src/components/modals/StudySelectionModal.jsx:87-93`**
`list.stats` is set to `(list.stats || {})`, so the `{list.stats && (...)}` guard is always true тАФ the "words learned" row always renders (a false impression of guarding). Functionally fine via `?? 0`.
**Fix:** Guard on a meaningful field (`typeof list.stats.wordsLearned === 'number'`) or drop the dead conditional.

---

## Systemic Patterns

These cut across many findings and warrant single, sweeping fixes rather than per-file patches:

1. **Non-atomic Firestore read-modify-write (TOCTOU).** `updateClassProgress` (progressService.js), `submitChallenge`/`reviewChallenge` (db.js), and `addWordToList`/`deleteWord`/wordCount updates (db.js) all do `getDoc тЖТ mutate in JS тЖТ updateDoc` with no transaction or batch, and several "guards" are check-then-act on the same stale snapshot. **Sweep:** adopt `runTransaction` for any counter/array/day-increment write, or use `FieldValue.increment` + idempotent doc keys; establish a convention that all denormalized-counter writes are batched with their source write.

2. **Client-side filtering applied after the pagination `limit`.** `queryTeacherAttempts` and `queryStudentAttempts` (db.js) compute `hasMore`/`lastVisible` on the raw page, then `continue`-filter тАФ surfacing as broken counts, sparse pages, and partial select-all in Gradebook. **Sweep:** push filters into the Firestore query (listId/testType/name where indexable) or implement a shared fill-page helper that pages until `pageSize` post-filter rows accumulate and bases `hasMore` on raw pages remaining.

3. **Unbounded full-collection reads + N+1 fan-out.** `getSegmentWords`/`getNewWords`/`getBlindSpotPool` (studyService.js) read the entire words subcollection then filter in JS; `fetchStudentStats`/`fetchStudentAggregateStats` read all study_states; `fetchStudentsProgressForClass` and `fetchStudentClasses` issue per-student/per-list getDocs; `ClassDetail` re-runs all of this on every class mutation. **Sweep:** add server-side `where('position', range)` / `where('listId','==',...)` filters, batch reads via `getAll`/`in`-queries or `collectionGroup`, and decouple roster loading from progress loading so mutations don't re-trigger the whole fan-out.

4. **Error paths that destroy or silently swallow in-progress work.** Full-page error takeovers wipe live tests (BlindSpotCheck), unreachable retry UI (TypedTest), missing `catch` causing unhandled rejections (Dashboard `loadStudentClasses`/`handleListSelection`, BlindSpotCheck/DailySessionFlow handlers, clipboard), and silent errorтЖТempty-state collapse (Settings `loadClasses`, db.js member-fetch). **Sweep:** standardize on inline/non-destructive error states that keep work mounted and retryable, reserve full-page errors for initial-load failures, always `.catch` async event handlers, and route transient reads through the existing `withRetry` helper instead of raw `getDoc`.

5. **Two coexisting data shapes handled inconsistently.** Legacy vs. new testId formats (`resetStudentProgress` misses legacy), legacy `assignedLists` array vs. `assignments` map (ClassDetail progress fetch), 0-based `currentStudyDay` vs. 1-based `expectedDay` (Dashboard off-by-one), and duplicated assignment-defaulting logic between `fetchClass` and `fetchStudentClasses` that has already drifted. **Sweep:** centralize listId-extraction, assignment-shape normalization, and day-index conversion into single shared helpers used everywhere, eliminating per-call-site divergence.

6. **The simulation/dev harness is a self-contained bug cluster.** `word.text` vs `word.word`, double-increment, ref-vs-state split-brain, infinite loop, stale-closure auto-swipe, uncleared timers, and dev-log id collisions all live behind `VITE_SIMULATION_MODE`. No production user impact, but the harness produces unreliable audit data. **Sweep:** treat the simulation harness as a single fix unit тАФ align it with the real submit path's field names and ref-based state, and centralize timer lifecycle management (track every timeout in a ref, clear in stop/reset/unmount).

7. **Leftover debug logging in hot paths.** Unconditional `console.log` of submit results/session metadata (MCQTest) and imported word contents (ImportWordsModal). **Sweep:** add a lint rule against bare `console.log` and a single debug-gating utility.
